title: Estimation of Glycine, Alanine and Lysine and their Metal Complexes with Chloramine-T
author: N. M. M. GOWDA
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Post-graduate Studies and Research in Chemistry, 

 Manasa Gangotri, University of Mysore, Mysore-570 006, India. 

  Manuscript received 16 June 1977, revised 14 October 1977, accepted 14 March 1978  

 Chloramine-T oxidizes glycine and lysine with a six-electron change in  p H 5.0 and 4.0 buffer media respectively, while alanine undergoes a four-electron oxidation with this reagent in  p H 5.5 buffer. The analytical methods are useful for the estimation of amino· acids and their metal complexes in solution, by back titration procedures. Para toluene sulphonamide, cyanates and nitriles have been detected among the reaction products.
